---
title: "Consumer Complaint Resolution Optimisation"
description: "Predicting high-risk financial complaints using NLP and machine learning."
author: "Ananya Mahadeva"
date: 2025-11-26
image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1551288049-bebda4e38f71"
categories: [Python, Analytics,Machine Learning, NLP]
---

## Project Overview

This project focuses on analysing large-scale consumer complaint data to identify inefficiencies and improve resolution processes.

Using advanced analytics and machine learning, the project aims to prioritise high-risk complaints and optimise operational decision-making.



## 📁 Dataset

```{python}
import pandas as pd

data = {
    "Complaint_Type": ["Loan", "Credit Card", "Mortgage", "Loan", "Mortgage", "Credit Card"],
    "Risk": [1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 0]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)
df
```
## 📈 Analysis

```{python}
risk_summary = df.groupby("Complaint_Type")["Risk"].mean()
risk_summary
```

## 📊 Visualization

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

risk_summary.plot(kind='bar')
plt.title("Average Risk by Complaint Type")
plt.ylabel("Risk Level")
plt.show()
```

## 🧠 Analytical Approach

This project used a **prescriptive analytics framework** to optimise complaint resolution.

The dataset consisted of over **61,000 consumer complaint records** sourced from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB).

### Key steps:

- Data cleaning and preprocessing  
- Feature engineering (time-based, risk-based features)  
- Text analysis using **TF-IDF** on complaint narratives  
- Creation of predictive variables  
- Binary classification model to predict monetary relief  

A **Voting Classifier (Gradient Boosting + Random Forest)** was used to improve prediction accuracy and robustness.


## 📊 Key Metric Calculation

The key KPI calculated in this project was the **probability of monetary relief**, used to prioritise complaints.


#  logic used in the model
data["resolution_probability"] = model.predict_proba(X)[:, 1]


## 📈 Results & Model Performance

The model demonstrated strong predictive performance:

- Accuracy: 77.4%  
- Precision: 86.1%  
- ROC-AUC: 0.794  

High precision was prioritised to ensure that flagged complaints were genuinely high-risk, reducing unnecessary workload for the resolution team.

## Key Insights

- Complaint risk varies significantly across product categories (e.g., credit cards vs loans)  
- Higher complaint volumes are associated with increased financial risk  
- Longer complaint narratives are more likely to result in monetary relief  
- Seasonal trends impact complaint volume and operational pressure  

These insights helped identify operational bottlenecks and prioritisation opportunities.

## Business Impact

The implementation of this model enables:

- Faster identification of high-risk complaints  
- Improved allocation of specialist resolution teams  
- Reduction in financial losses due to delayed action  

A simulation estimated:

- Net Benefit: ~$470,000  
- ROI: ~1191%  

This demonstrates significant operational and financial value from data-driven decision making.

## Solution & Deployment

The final solution was deployed as an interactive Streamlit dashboard, allowing:

- Real-time complaint risk scoring  
- Decision support for resolution teams  
- Monitoring of operational performance  

The system enables a shift from reactive complaint handling to proactive risk management.
